article.title: Noizekid: A Profile of the Mexican Moombahton and Dancehall Producer
article.subtitle: Collaborations with DJ Snake, Bad Bunny, and Major Lazer define his trajectory.
article.author: D. Varela
article.excerpt: Noizekid stands as a prominent figure in the Mexican electronic music scene, recognized for his work in moombahton and dancehall. His collaborations with major industry names and the release of his sample pack underscore his influence.

content.text: Noizekid is a Mexican moombahton and dancehall producer known for his collaborations with prominent figures in the music industry, including DJ Snake, Bad Bunny, and Major Lazer. His work is characterized by a fusion of electronic beats with dancehall rhythms, establishing him as a key contributor to the evolving landscape of Latin electronic music.

In addition to his production work, Noizekid has released the 'Noizekid Sample Pack' in collaboration with Barnation. This collection includes a comprehensive set of samples, loops, and drums that he has utilized in various productions, offering insight into his creative process and sound design techniques.

His approach to production is marked by a meticulous attention to detail, blending traditional dancehall elements with modern electronic textures. This fusion has earned him recognition within both the Latin music scene and the broader electronic dance music community.

Noizekid's collaborations with artists such as ETC!ETC!, Los Dutis, LOOJAN, Bsno, and Freebot further highlight his role as a connector within the industry. These partnerships have contributed to a cross-pollination of styles and techniques, enriching the overall sound of contemporary electronic music.

While his discography and track listings remain largely unindexed, Noizekid's influence is evident through his sample pack and the widespread recognition of his production work. His contributions continue to shape the direction of moombahton and dancehall, cementing his status as an important figure in the genre.
